private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Swarm Aero is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $8M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for advancement from Seed to next-stage funding, yielding $20M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median across these four peers is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The two methods produce substantially divergent results. Stage-progression implies $20M; peer-median implies $225M. Triangulating these approaches yields a valuation range of $17M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $122M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty about which comparables most closely reflect Swarm Aero's market position and growth trajectory. The stage-progression method assumes limited margin expansion at Seed stage, while peer-median is sensitive to the inclusion of higher-valued Series A firms. Resolution of this range requires clearer visibility into Swarm Aero's revenue run rate, unit economics, and market traction relative to peer benchmarks.
